A novel image analysis methodology for o.title: A novel image analysis methodology for online monitoring of nucleation and crystal growth during solid state phase transformations

d2cs00172a.pdf.title: Recent progress in the development of fluorescent probes for imaging pathological oxidative stress
d2cs00172a.pdf.abstract: Oxidative stress is closely related to the physiopathology of numerous diseases. Reactive oxygen species (ROS), reactive nitrogen species (RNS), and reactive sulfur species (RSS) are direct participants and important biomarkers of oxidative stress.

Статья 3.pdf.title: Preparation of fibrous polymer grafted magnetic chitosan beads and modified with hydrazone and imidazole groups: isolation of U(VI) ions from seawater
Статья 3.pdf.journal: Journal of Radioanalytical and Nuclear Chemistry
Статья 3.pdf.abstract: The magnetic chitosan beads were prepared and grafted with glycidylmethacrylate, then diaminomelanonitrile (DAMN) was attached. The nitrile groups of DAMN were reacted with hydrazine hydrate hydrochloride and hydrazine hydrate-ethylene diamine to obtain hydrazine (HYD) and imidazole (IMDZ) groups, respectively. Both adsorbents removed uranyl ions from the aqueous medium and seawater. The adsorption capacities of the Fe₃O₄@CHT@p(GMA)-HYD and Fe₃O₄@CHT@p(GMA)-IMDZ, beads were 328.4 and 434.7 mg/g, respectively. Both adsorbents have high performance for adsorbing U(VI) ions from seawater. The synthesized adsorbents were used to isolate U(VI) ions from seawater in the presence of various ions.
